mission.title: dapr: Resiliency - Service Invocation allow to ignore resiliency policy based on response http status code
mission.description: Resiliency - Service Invocation allow to ignore resiliency policy based on response http status code. Requested by 6+ users.

steps.title: Check current dapr deployment
steps.description:
Verify your dapr version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n dapr -l app.kubernetes.io/name=dapr
helm list -n dapr 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working dapr installation.

steps.title: Review dapr configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant dapr configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n dapr -l app.kubernetes.io/name=dapr
kubectl get configmap -n dapr -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=dapr
```
## In what area(s)?

## Describe the feature
In a business case it might not be required to always execute the resiliency policy if a http status code indicates a business error and it isn't expected that a retry will solve this.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Resiliency - Service Invocation allow to ignore resiliency…
steps.description:
I believe what the author means is if we apply the configuration below

```
apiVersion: dapr.io/v1alpha1
kind: Resiliency
metadata:
  name: slotting-pubsub-resiliency
  namespace: apps
scopes:
spec:
  policies:
    timeouts:
      general: 5s
      important: 60s
      largeResponse: 10s
   
```yaml
apiVersion: dapr.io/v1alpha1
kind: Resiliency
metadata:
  name: slotting-pubsub-resiliency
  namespace: apps
scopes:
spec:
  policies:
    timeouts:
      general: 5s
      important: 60s
      largeResponse: 10s
    retries:
      linerRetry:
        policy: constant
        duration: 5s
        maxRetries: 3
        retryOn:
          - 500
          - 503
        IgnoredOn:
          - 4**
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n dapr -l app.kubernetes.io/name=dapr
kubectl get events -n dapr --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Resiliency - Service Invocation allow to ignore resiliency…" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
I believe what the author means is if we apply the configuration below

```
apiVersion: dapr.io/v1alpha1
kind: Resiliency
metadata:
  name: slotting-pubsub-resiliency
  namespace: apps
scopes:
spec:
  policies:
    timeouts:
      general: 5s
      important: 60s
      largeResponse: 10s
    retries:
      linerRetry:
        policy: constant
        duration: 5s
        maxRetries: 3

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with dapr installed or the issue environment reproducible.
